contentBlocks.original:
यातायातप्रशमनी यातनानान्निकृन्तनी ।
योगावासा योगिवन्द्या यत्तच्छब्दस्वरूपिणी ॥ 137 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yātāyātapraśamanī yātanānānnikṛntanī ।
yōgāvāsā yōgivandyā yattachChabdasvarūpiṇī ॥ 137 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yatayataprashamani (calmer of comings and goings), Yataanaaninikritanani (cutter of many afflictions), Yogavasa (abode of yoga), Yogivandya (worshipped by yogis), Yattachabdasvarupini (of the form of the restrained/controlled word). (137)

contentBlocks.original:
योगक्षेममयी यन्त्रा यावदक्षरमातृका ।
यावत्पदमयी यावच्छब्दरूपा यथेश्वरी ॥ 138 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgakṣēmamayī yantrā yāvadakṣaramātṛkā ।
yāvatpadamayī yāvachChabdarūpā yathēśvarī ॥ 138 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yogakshhemamayi (pervaded by yoga and kshema/security), Yantra (the yantra), Yavadakshramaatrika (the mother of all syllables up to the last), Yavadpadamayi (pervaded by all words/padas), Yavacchhabdarupa (of the form of all speech), Yatheshvari (sovereign as she wills). (138)

contentBlocks.original:
यत्तदीया यक्षवन्द्या यद्विद्या यतिसंस्तुता ।
यावद्विद्यामयी यावद्विद्याबृन्दसुवन्दिता ॥ 139 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yattadīyā yakṣavandyā yadvidyā yatisaṃstutā ।
yāvadvidyāmayī yāvadvidyābṛndasuvanditā ॥ 139 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yattadiya (belonging to the one who restrains), Yakshavandya (worshipped by yakshas), Yadvidya (knowledge itself), Yatisamstuta (praised by ascetics), Yavadvidyaamayi (pervaded by all knowledge), Yavadvidyabrindasuvandita (well-worshipped by all circles of knowledge). (139)

contentBlocks.original:
योगिहृत्पद्मनिलया योगिवर्यप्रियङ्करी ।
योगिवन्द्या योगिमाता योगीशफलदायिनी ॥ 140 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgihṛtpadmanilayā yōgivaryapriyaṅkarī ।
yōgivandyā yōgimātā yōgīśaphaladāyinī ॥ 140 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yogihritpadmanilaya (dwelling in the lotus-hearts of yogis), Yogivaryapriyankari (beloved of the best of yogis), Yogivandya (worshipped by yogis), Yogimata (mother of yogis), Yogishaphaladayini (bestower of fruits on the lord of yogis). (140)

contentBlocks.original:
यक्षवन्द्या यक्षपूज्या यक्षराजसुपूजिता ।
यज्ञरूपा यज्ञतुष्टा यायजूकस्वरूपिणी ॥ 141 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yakṣavandyā yakṣapūjyā yakṣarājasupūjitā ।
yajñarūpā yajñatuṣṭā yāyajūkasvarūpiṇī ॥ 141 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yakshavandya (worshipped by yakshas), Yakshapujya (offered worship by yakshas), Yakshaaraajasupujita (well-worshipped by the king of yakshas), Yajnarupa (of the form of sacrifice), Yajnatushtta (satisfied by sacrifice), Yayajukasvarupini (of the form of the sacrificer). (141)

contentBlocks.original:
यन्त्राराध्या यन्त्रमध्या यन्त्रकर्तृप्रियङ्करी ।
यन्त्रारूढा यन्त्रपूज्या योगिध्यानपरायणा ॥ 142 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yantrārādhyā yantramadhyā yantrakartṛpriyaṅkarī ।
yantrārūḍhā yantrapūjyā yōgidhyānaparāyaṇā ॥ 142 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yantraradhya (worshipped through the yantra), Yantramadhya (dwelling in the centre of the yantra), Yantrakartripriyankari (beloved of yantra-makers), Yantraruda (ascended on the yantra), Yantrapujya (worthy of yantra-worship), Yogidhyanaaparayana (devoted to the meditation of yogis). (142)

contentBlocks.original:
यजनीया यमस्तुत्या योगयुक्ता यशस्करी ।
योगबद्धा यतिस्तुत्या योगज्ञा योगनायकी ॥ 143 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yajanīyā yamastutyā yōgayuktā yaśaskarī ।
yōgabaddhā yatistutyā yōgajñā yōganāyakī ॥ 143 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yajaniya (worthy of worship), Yamasttutya (praised by Yama), Yogayukta (united with yoga), Yashaskarini (cause of fame), Yogabaddha (bound in yoga), Yatisttutya (praised by ascetics), Yogajna (knower of yoga), Yoganayaki (guide of yoga). (143)

contentBlocks.original:
योगिज्ञानप्रदा यक्षी यमबाधाविनाशिनी ।
योगिकाम्यप्रदात्री च योगिमोक्षप्रदायिनी ॥ 144 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgijñānapradā yakṣī yamabādhāvināśinī ।
yōgikāmyapradātrī cha yōgimōkṣapradāyinī ॥ 144 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yogijnapraaprada (bestower of knowledge on yogis), Yakshi (a yaksha goddess), Yamabaadhaavinashini (destroyer of the afflictions of Yama), Yogikamyapradatri (giver of what yogis desire), Yogimokshapradayini (bestower of liberation on yogis). (144)

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुतिः
इति नाम्नां सरस्वत्याः सहस्रं समुदीरितम् ।
मन्त्रात्मकं महागोप्यं महासारस्वतप्रदम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśrutiḥ
iti nāmnāṃ sarasvatyāḥ sahasraṃ samudīritam ।
mantrātmakaṃ mahāgōpyaṃ mahāsārasvatapradam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Phalashruti: Thus the thousand names of Sarasvati have been recited — mantra-filled, greatly secret, bestowing supreme eloquence and learning. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्भक्त्यात्त्रिकालं साधकः पुमान् ।
सर्वविद्यानिधिः साक्षात् स एव भवति ध्रुवम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādbhaktyāttrikālaṃ sādhakaḥ pumān ।
sarvavidyānidhiḥ sākṣāt sa ēva bhavati dhruvam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites or hears these names with devotion, three times daily — that person truly and certainly becomes a direct treasury of all knowledge. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
लभते सम्पदः सर्वाः पुत्रपौत्रादिसंयुताः ।
मूकोऽपि सर्वविद्यासु चतुर्मुख इवापरः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labhatē sampadaḥ sarvāḥ putrapautrādisaṃyutāḥ ।
mūkō'pi sarvavidyāsu chaturmukha ivāparaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He attains all wealth together with sons and grandsons. Even if mute, he becomes as eloquent as Brahma the four-faced one — a second Brahma — in all branches of knowledge. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
भूत्वा प्राप्नोति सान्निध्यं अन्ते धातुर्मुनीश्वर ।
सर्वमन्त्रमयं सर्वविद्यामानफलप्रदम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūtvā prāpnōti sānnidhyaṃ antē dhāturmunīśvara ।
sarvamantramayaṃ sarvavidyāmānaphalapradam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having placed this garland of names upon his head, at the end of life he attains the proximity of Brahma the creator. It contains all mantras and bestows the fruit of all knowledge. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
महाकवित्वदं पुंसां महासिद्धिप्रदायकम् ।
कस्मै चिन्न प्रदातव्यं प्राणैः कण्ठगतैरपि ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahākavitvadaṃ puṃsāṃ mahāsiddhipradāyakam ।
kasmai chinna pradātavyaṃ prāṇaiḥ kaṇṭhagatairapi ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: It bestows great poetic power on people and great spiritual accomplishment. This should not be given to just anyone — guard it even at the cost of one's life. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
महारहस्यं सततं वाणीनामसहस्रकम् ।
सुसिद्धमस्मदादीनां स्तोत्रं ते समुदीरितम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahārahasyaṃ satataṃ vāṇīnāmasahasrakam ।
susiddhamasmadādīnāṃ stōtraṃ tē samudīritam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This is an eternally great secret — the thousand names of Vani, the goddess of speech. This hymn, well-perfected among the likes of us, has been recited to you. (6)

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीस्कान्दपुराणान्तर्गत श्रीसनत्कुमार संहितायां नारद सनत्कुमार संवादे श्री सरस्वती सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīskāndapurāṇāntargata śrīsanatkumāra saṃhitāyāṃ nārada sanatkumāra saṃvādē śrī sarasvatī sahasranāma stōtraṃ sampūrṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Sri Sarasvati Sahasranama Stotram, found in the Sri Skanda Purana, in the Sri Sanatkumara Samhita, in the dialogue between Narada and Sanatkumara.
